item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just love Nathan Pierce's reaction to learning that his creation was used on people: "Why, why would you turn a joke into a torture device? Just… just get out of my cell. I'm done talking."

This is just so typical of the Foundation's need to know EVERYTHING, and how it just fucks thing up, and probably would be the reaction of a typical citizen if they ever found out about the Foundation, and what they have done, and continue to do. "I'm done talking to you. I want my phone call, and my lawyer".

What would they ever do without amnestics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just discovered this and it's already one of my favourites. It's just a simple concept explored in great detail, which is how I like my SCPs. It can be described in one sentence, and the article exists to rationalise this explanation and explore the implications and edge cases. Ok, "it's a gun that makes things worse", but what does that actually mean in practice? This article answers that question beautifully, whilst also poking fun at the fact that, in trying to answer these questions, the foundation has caused more harm than this SCP ever would have if just left alone (the ending of the log is brilliant). The humour is also refreshing, especially since it makes sense in the context of the SCP (it's a literal joke).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what typically goes into making something like this. Like what exactly did he do to give it those properties? Could he just as easily have made something that has the opposite effect? I wonder what the limits are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's like saying a gun manufacturer should be held responsible when someone uses a gun they made for a school shooting. Or that a car manufacturer should be held responsible if someone drives into a crowd. In both of those cases, responsibility lies solely with the person who actually used it to do something bad.

Even if you disagree with me on that, they still had no excuse to deprive him of his freedom. Financial liability for accidental harm at most.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There can be funny articles as normal SCPs. To determine whether something should be a Joke article, consider whether the article could be real. This usually works, although I will admit not all work with this rule.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should've been a -J to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally, a solution to 682. I really liked how you showed just arrogant and heartless the Foundation can be. It's cathartic seeing such arrogant people, like that researcher, being put in their place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am very sad that this doesn't have extended logs, this would be perfect for extended logs, especially since the base log really sold it for me above anything else in this article.

Oh well, still mega cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know, I have to agree with the Foundation on this one.

Think about it like this: imagine if the Foundation didn't exist. The kid makes his gun and gives it to his brother. What if the brother snaps and uses it on kids at his school? What if a prank goes wrong and someone gets hurt? What if someone steals it? Or one of the other dozen GoI gets wind of a gun that turns things into worse versions of themselves and decides they want it?

Not intending harm doesn't make you not culpable for creating something obviously dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great concept and an entertaining test log for the nerfing anomaly. (I would love to see this used against other anomalies but I doubt that could happen)
+1

item.title: Re: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: its a modded pic of a nerf strongarm pistol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some small grammar mistakes.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually scary how many times you can hear people in normal speech saying stuff like "lol" or "kek" or pretty much any internet slang. I've heard all the stuff in the article and worse just on the train to work and back. Generally from people who seem to be either younger or around the same age as Pierce is said to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A in-short test log:

Item: #SCP-3108

Tissue test record: overridden by O5 command.

Result:
D-80703 was released into temporary containment sector 12 equipped with SCP-3108. SCP-682 was promptly released into the chamber afterwards. D-80703 fired SCP-3108 at SCP-682. No effect was observed. D-80703 was then mauled by SCP-682, leaving SCP-3108 unharmed. At this point, the experiment was aborted, and MTF Eta-6 (whom was onsite at the time) was mobilized to retrieve SCP-3108 and SCP-3108-1. SCP-682's front left leg siezed SCP-3108 and managed to fire it at a nearby wall, turning it into heavily rotted wood. SCP-682 Smashed through the wall, breaching containment and killing ██ personnel before being re-contained by MTF Eta-6.
Note: it seems SCP-682 is not affected by SCP-3108. More testing will be required -Sr. Researcher Fred

item.title: Re: +200!!!
item.content:encoded: Grats. I'd advise writing a 3108-682 termination test. Overridden by O5, of course.
Wow, this is a huge milestone for me as an author here. Thanks everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't sure after the first few GAW skips I read, but this skip has made me start to like GAW as a concept. Good job!

(Poor D-Class)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't such a supplement ruin the point of the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to the accuracy of SCP-3108 being in line with similar products, a maximum one (1) meter distance is to be maintained from the intended target to prevent accidents.

do you mean this by being very accurate or not accurate? just wondering because normal nerf darts aren't very accurate. also I have a idea: maybe make a supplement like 914 has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems I've a new favourite skip, eh?

This was absolutely beautiful, hilarious and well written!

Also Coke is better than Pepsi and you'll never convince me otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
POI-6897: That, um, that isn't necessary. I'm sure he's a busy guy, we wouldn't want to bother him, lol.

Dr. Dietz: Pierce, did you really just say the letters lol out loud?

+1 just for this, holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the end where he grew genuinely mad the Foundation used the gun to harm people. It was a surprising touch of heart to an otherwise comedy-focused article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep in mind that this guy is a teenager, one who has access to powerful anomalous abilities. People his age usually believe they're invincible, so imagine how much worse it would be in his case. His language sort of fits with my headcanon of GAW being a group of 4chanesque internet outcasts and wannabes, who act random, edgy, or irritating just to seem cool. I have sadly encountered actual people using words like those online and in real life, so the interview didn't feel too out of place for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We could try using the gun on Keter skips, but it'd probably just turn them humanoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the gun, but I'm ultimately novoting, largely because of the interview. I can't believe that a shitty troll captured by the Foundation would be saying "lol" or tossing "kek" and "fascist cuck" around. On that note, regardless of why he chooses to say things like that, I don't think GAW would want to have him. "Get out of my cell, I'm done talking" is also an awfully bold statement from someone in his position, and all his lines that are more than a sentence or so long feel unnatural (that last bit is true of the doctor, as well).

In my opinion, this would be a much stronger entry without the interview, but it seems I'm in the minority there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huge thanks to VolgunStrife for doing a great reading of this skip!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I can really sympathize with that guy who created this SCP. He just innocently created it for fun one day, and he ends up having it stolen from him by a powerful government organization, and getting kidnapped and forced to have a not-so-pleasant talk with a member of said organization about it. There, he not only finds out they've been using his creation to do terrible things, but to add insult to injury, they start berating him and accusing him, treating it as a given that he himself would want to do evil stuff with it, and acting as if, even besides that, he had somehow done wrong simply by creating it for his own enjoyment.

I know the Foundation would never do this, but Jesus Christ, apologize to this man, and give him back his fucking toy. Like he said, he made it for fun. Now get off his back about it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did it ever occur to him to make one with the opposite effect? Is there any reason he couldn't?

item.title: Re: It is Nerf or Nothing!
item.content:encoded: Honestly the Coke to Pepsi joke was largely what propelled this from random brainstorming into an actual article. I loved the joke and really wanted to see it come alive.

item.title: It is Nerf or Nothing!
item.content:encoded: This SCP is weird like honestly it is a simple concept which supposedly should be funny and serious at the same time but due to it's simplicity and me guessing almost all the jokes it just became another one of those SCPs they probably hold in a storage room or something(that isn't a bad thing per say but this feels like it would become more dustier in the shelves than the others in the same storage room if you get my meaning) however the joke about Coke and Pepsi did manage to get a laugh out of me and the interview actually was well done enough to end it satisfyingly so overall I guess I'll give it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview alone is enough for me to upvote, lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because the assumption is that the PoI is applying the knowledge of how to make SCPs, not that they are willingly generating SCPs that nobody else can make.

If you have a spellbook that allows anyone reading it to cast spells, the book is the SCP, not the person. Even if someone memorizes the spells in the book.

Even if they write a new spellbook with new spells created from what they learned from the old spellbook, the book is gonna be the SCP with the person as a PoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love this but why isn't the POI an SCP?
Thanks a bunch everybody!

item.title: Upvoted
item.content:encoded: I really like the characterization that last interview log gives, especially since it shines a light on the darker part of foundation testing. After all, what did they think would happen if they shot a human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like it, though I find one point to be a bit dissonant. The Ethics Committee stymied any more testing on humans due to the possibility of creating some anomalous hominid, which makes sense. However, it seems like human testing would probably be something they didn't deem necessary and therefore unethical in the first place.

NONETHELESS, I somehow feel like this adds some impact at the end. And even though I think it's odd they would allow human testing at all, it still doesn't seem entirely out of character. This makes me imagine that at some point, the research team assigned to it actually came up with a compelling argument for why they should be allowed to.

All that said, I like the concept behind it, I love the fact that it was made basically for teh lulz, and it's nice to see someone calling out the Foundation for their BS. +1, would upvote again if could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, the interview was my least favourite part. But if the kid is meant to be unlikable then I guess that was done well.

I enjoyed the experiment log, especially the ones where the results are just, like, your opinion man.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I lol'd at the article, and I was thoroughly gut-punched by the end of the interview taking seriously the reprehensible torture/test that I rolled over lightly because of how I was disarmed by the earlier comedy. You get my +1 and an Audio Adaptation!

https://youtu.be/a1W0sxPqFcs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me after "OP PLZ NERF", "Kektagon" and "ME2>ME3". I can even forgive you being wrong on "cats vs dogs" case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a crosslink to the site 64 hub

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kektagon is basically a low key troll with no ideology save live and let live. If he thinks he can annoy someone he doesn't like, he'll do it. The fact that his anachronistic name and lingo feels vexing to you is pretty much his intentional vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the pun, but it is irrationally bothering me that a guy who throws "cuck" around while having "kek" in his username is a member of the extraordinary left GAW.

I guess he could be operating behind 10 layers of irony but that's kind of a weird priority to have when you're being interrogated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't they try to make human SCPs generally happy, like when they removed the tattoo from Mr. Hax?

Only if making them happy makes them easier to control.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta love the way you subtly express your opinion (Doggos>cats)
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was an error on the researcher's part. He was fed up with Kektagon's crap and let it slip while he was frustrated. Also, kekt isn't a scp, just a person of interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why the doctor told Kektagon that he used the gun on a person. It would cause unnecessary distress to the guy, and a desire to escape or harm Foundation staff. Don't they try to make human SCPs generally happy, like when they removed the tattoo from Mr. Hax?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah alright, thanks for the explanation. It just sounded like a surname that'd be in some -J article test log personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked the interview, my favorite part of this thing.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is… is that a nerf gun?

"SCP-3108 is a heavily modified Nerf brand dart gun"

Right, okay, reading on…

"OP PLS NERF"

Welp, that's a +1.

*Finishes reading*

"Trying to re rate this article?"

Oh, right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was probably just going to upvote because of "It's Nerf or Nothing" but when kektagon called it a "nerfing gun" it was just too much. +1

P.S. this kinda reminded me of a reverse 914

item.title: Re: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: Yes. And the picture used is a modded NERF gun as well.

item.title: Re: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: So SCP-3108 is a modded NERF gun? I see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that picture was a modified nerf gun. And then the anomaly was too. Neat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha, I'll swap the link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3090 is no longer a lethal item. SCP-2433 is, though.

DonDeLillo: someone fucking died last week in michigan because one appeared in his lungs you dumbass

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you wound up liking it in the end! As for Deets, it's an anglicization of the German name Diez. https://www.houseofnames.com/deets-family-crest

Not the most common name ever, but it definitely exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically. Site 64 in canon deals with a lot of GAW and AWCY, and this wasn't the first time pierce had been interviewed. The researchers were fed up with nonsense by this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh Pierce is a complete hypocrite. He's basically an internet troll that makes anomalous items out of junk. Thinking ahead is something he doesn't tend to do.
